#capecoralrealestate #swflrealestate #floridahomesforsale #movingtoflorida #relocationspecialist
Cape Coral_FL.script: This $650K Cape Coral home just broke my brain a little. Four bedrooms, three bathrooms, nearly 2,000 square feet and it sits on a freshwater canal. Walk in and you get soaring ceilings, built in speakers and a kitchen island that honestly looks too good to cook on. There is a built in bar, a master suite with a walk in closet the size of a small apartment, and a shower with FOUR heads. Then step outside to your covered lanai, outdoor kitchen, pool and extra entertaining space. This is the lifestyle people move to Florida FOR. And here is the thing, homes like this do not sit long in Cape Coral right now. Buyers are moving fast and inventory is tightening every week. So tell me honestly, what is the one feature in this home that would make you pull the trigger TODAY?

Cape Coral_FL.script: Here's what $680,000 actually gets you in Cape Coral right now. Impact windows, open floor plan, kitchen island, a pool out back with a covered lanai and 90-degree glass sliders. The master suite has dual sinks, a soaking tub, and a walk-through shower. Two more bedrooms on the other side, a second full bath, and the whole thing sits at 1,857 square feet. And here's what most buyers don't realize: at this price point in Cape Coral, you're still getting new construction quality finishes that would cost $900,000 or more in Naples or Sarasota right now. The gap between those markets is closing fast, and Cape Coral is the last window where this level of home at this price still exists. So tell me, are you buying in Cape Coral this year or are you still on the fence? Drop it below and I'll send you everything available in this price range this week.

Cape Coral_FL.script:
Okay real talk... this Cape Coral waterfront home won't solve ALL your problems.

But waking up to this view every single morning? That one hits different.

Direct Gulf access. Your own private dock. Sunsets that make your neighbors back home genuinely jealous.

I keep coming back to this one because honestly it feels less like a house and more like a permanent vacation you actually own.

Cape Coral has over 400 miles of canals and most people have no idea homes like this are still available at these prices right now.

The people who bought here three years ago? They are not complaining.

This lifestyle is real and it is closer than you think.

So tell me honestly... if mornings looked like this for you, what would you actually do with your boat on day one?
